title: Error Handling Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of the 4-argument global error middleware and the role of wrapping asynchronous handlers to catch uncaught awaited errors.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily role of 'Error Handling' in an Express.js application?
options: Catching and managing exceptions to prevent the server from crashing and to provide professional feedback to users.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: In Express.js, how many arguments should an 'Error Middleware' function always contain?
Which and/or what was the primarily benefit of using a specialized utility function like 'asyncHandler'?
options: To automatically catch errors in an asynchronous route and pass them to the global error middleware.

questions.question: Which argument in the Error Middleware contains the specific error message or object passed from the previous code?
Does following a solid 'Error Handling' strategy help build truly Fullstack applications by ensuring high availability and professional user feedback during crashes?
options: Yes, as it is a core feature of any modern server-side framework.
